DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF CARL WESCOTT'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO RE-FILING. THE MOVING PARTY FAILED TO ATTACH TO HIS DECLARATION THE DISCOVERY PLEADINGS TO WHICH RESPONSES ARE SOUGHT ALONG WITH PROOFS OF SERVICE OF SAID PLEADINGS. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM. THE PROPOSED ORDER MAY BE SUBMITTED ELECTRONICALLY TO JUDGE PRO TEM STEVEN B. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-13-2022 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. There being no appearance the court orders the case dismissed pursuant to Government Code section 68608(b). The court finds no good cause or substantial justification for failure to comply with the previous court orders. A less severe sanction would not be effective due to the history of lack of compliance. JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; Clerk: J. Rumsey; Proceedings not reported.

Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Imposing Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-15-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Carl Wescott, T:415.335.5000, plaintiff/moving party appearing on his own behalf;
No other appearance.

Plaintiff stipulated to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING: There being no objection, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF CARL WESCOTT's Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Imposing Monetary Sanctions.

The Motion is granted as follows: The truth of the facts set forth in Request for Admissions, Set One served on Mr. Middlemiss are deemed admitted. Since Mr. Wescott did not incur any legal fees in filing this motion, sanctions (or if he did such fees are not disclosed in his declaration) are not authorized.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge Pro Tem: Thomas Cohen; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Not reported.

Plaintiff Carl Wescott'S Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery
Off Calendar Oct-31-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Carl A. Wescott, In Pro Per Law Student (415-335-5000) appears as self-represented plaintiff/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

Plaintiff Carl Wescott'S Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery is DENIED without prejudice to re-filing.
